— —

methodology

  • Payload: benchmarks/cross_binding/results/full_matrix.csv
  • Rows: algorithm × dataset size × thread count
  • Columns: C++ ABI, public bindings, and external reference libraries
  • Cell tooltip: raw parity, binding parity, reference parity, and reason

See the methodology page for the policy before publishing results.

host

backends

Internal columns are nirs4all-methods routes. External columns are third-party or language-standard reference methods.

parity verdict

Internal binding rows compare against the direct C++ ABI. Reference gates compare native C++ and comparator rows against stored external-reference snapshots.

  • ✓ exact - result within tolerance
  • ◇ context - not parity-gated because the external method has a different contract
  • ? candidate - external implementation identified, but a same-contract adapter is still blocked
  • ⊘ reference needed - no executable same-contract external reference has been found yet
  • ≈ drift - result outside exact tolerance
  • ✗ divergent - result outside drift threshold
  • ! error - dependency, runtime, crash or exception
  • · not run - no timing was produced
  • ∅ not available in lib - the library does not expose that operator

The reference column lists the external library or formula used for each method when one is available.
